frameworks.name: default-mode network
frameworks.one_line: resting-state DMN intrinsic connectivity shapes individual vicarious-reward responsiveness

frameworks.name: vicarious reward
frameworks.one_line: vicarious experience of others' positive sensations indexed by beta-band oscillations

core_question: Does resting-state functional connectivity within the default-mode network predict individual differences in the magnitude of vicarious reward responses?
key_assumptions: beta oscillatory activity indexes vicarious reward magnitude
intra-DMN connectivity at rest reflects stable trait-like organization
individual differences in empathy have a measurable neural correlate
manipulations: across-subject variation in DMN intra-network connectivity
key_findings: DMN intra-network resting connectivity positively predicts beta-band vicarious-reward response magnitude across N=30 adults
interpretations: DMN resting-state organization shapes individual differences in vicarious reward
